content.problemStatement: Rectify the errors to make the program compile successfully
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases (1 ≤ T ≤ 10). No further input is required for each test case.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the string "121212" on a separate line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: T = 1, so the program prints the constant string once.
T = 3, the constant string is printed three times, each on a new line.
T = 5, the string is printed five times.

solution.approach: Read the integer T. Loop exactly T times and print the constant string "121212" followed by a newline. The problem does not require any computation beyond repeated output, so the algorithm runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time print per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few scalar variables are used.
